summary: Strong first set with Jack Straw & Althea, second set uneven.
blurb: The show opens tightly with Let It Grow, Jack Straw, Althea and Queen Jane Approximately, delivering solid jams on Loser and Tennessee Jed. Highlights include a fiery Truckin' > New Speedway Boogie transition and an emotional Standing On The Moon in the second set, though the latter half drags with weaker performances of Uncle John’s Band and Corrina.
review:
July 13, 1994 at Franklin County Airport captures a Grateful Dead night that swings between brilliance and stumble. The first set is a bright spot – Jerry’s lead work on Jack Straw shines with a “phenomenal” tone, Althea rolls out smoothly, and the band locks into Let It Grow and Tennessee Jed with crisp, energetic playing. The soundboard recording praised as “great soundboard” captures these moments cleanly, despite occasional speaker drop‑outs.

The second set is more mixed. A high‑octane Truckin' > New Speedway Boogie transition fuels the crowd, and Jerry’s eye‑contact during Standing On The Moon moves listeners to tears, but the momentum fizzles as Uncle John’s Band suffers lyrical flubs and Corrina drags. Vince Welnick’s keyboard antics draw mixed reactions – some fans love his shirt‑off stage presence while others hear him out of sync. Overall, the show offers enough standout moments (Jack Straw, Althea, Standing On The Moon) to merit a listen, especially via the recommended soundboard source.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, one mixed and one negative; consensus points to a solid first set with standout songs but a weaker second half, yielding a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard capture of the first set highlights; most complete and widely praised despite minor speaker drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Jack Straw & Truckin' > New Speedway Boogie transition energizes the first half.
Emotional Standing On The Moon moment with Jerry’s eye contact.
Strong opening set featuring Althea, Let It Grow and tight band interplay.
Second‑set drag – Uncle John’s Band lyrical flubs and Corrina feel sluggish.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers dynamic lead work on Jack Straw and an emotional eye‑contact moment on Standing On The Moon, though occasional lyric flubs and a tired vocal tone are noted.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal delivery is uneven; some lines are flubbed, especially during Uncle John’s Band.
band_performance.Brent: Vince Welnick draws mixed reactions – his shirt‑off antics amuse fans while his playing is described as sloshed or out of sync by others.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides steady drumming foundation with no notable issues reported.
band_performance.Billy: Solid percussion throughout; no major performance complaints noted.
